foldable_bicycles.peak_hour_rule: Foldable bicycles are NOT permitted during peak hours even if folded.
foldable_bicycles.placement: Passenger is responsible for keeping the bicycle with them and must not block aisles or doors.

standard_bicycles.reason: Standard (non-foldable) bicycles are not permitted on the metro at any time.

electric_scooters.reason: E-scooters and hoverboards are prohibited on all metro services due to fire safety regulations, even if folded or switched off.

luggage.oversized_peak_rule: Items exceeding the size limit are not permitted during peak hours (07:00–09:30 and 17:00–20:00 weekdays).
luggage.placement: All luggage must be held by the passenger or placed in the luggage area at the ends of the carriage. Luggage must not block seats, aisles, or doors.

pets.conditions: Small pets in fully enclosed hard or soft carriers only.
pets.seat_occupancy: Pets must not occupy a seat.
pets.exceptions: Guide dogs and registered assistance animals are permitted at all times without a carrier.
pets.prohibited: Dogs not in carriers are not permitted on the metro, including on leads.

food_and_drink.eating: Eating is discouraged on the metro. Passengers must not leave litter.
food_and_drink.alcohol: Alcohol consumption is not permitted on metro services or at stations.

priority_seating.description: Seats marked with a yellow priority sign are reserved for elderly passengers (65+), pregnant women, passengers with disabilities, and passengers travelling with young children.
priority_seating.rule: All other passengers must vacate priority seats immediately when requested.

prohibited_items: Items emitting strong odours that may cause discomfort to other passengers
metro.smoking_and_vaping: Strictly prohibited on all metro carriages, platforms, and station concourses.
metro.noise: Passengers are asked to keep noise to a considerate level. Use of personal audio devices with external speakers is not permitted.

foldable_bicycles.placement: Must be stored in the overhead rack or in the space at the end of the carriage.

standard_bicycles.conditions: Standard bicycles are permitted in designated bicycle bays only. Maximum 2 bicycles per train.
standard_bicycles.peak_hour_rule: Standard bicycles are NOT permitted on national rail services during peak hours.
standard_bicycles.booking: Bicycle spaces are first-come-first-served and cannot be reserved in advance. Fee is payable at the platform bicycle bay gate.
standard_bicycles.placement: Bicycles must be placed in the designated bicycle bay. Passengers remain responsible for their bicycle.

electric_scooters.reason: E-scooters and hoverboards are not permitted on national rail services.

luggage.overhead_rack: Items must fit in the overhead luggage rack above seats. No formal size restriction, but items must not cause inconvenience to other passengers.
luggage.large_items: Oversized items (e.g. large suitcases) should be placed in the dedicated luggage areas at the ends of each carriage.
luggage.notes: Passengers are responsible for their own luggage at all times. The operator is not liable for lost or damaged luggage.

pets.guide_and_assistance_dogs: Permitted at all times in all coaches at no charge.

other_dogs.conditions: Dogs must be on a lead at all times. Permitted in standard class coaches only.

small_animals_in_carriers.conditions: Small animals in enclosed carriers permitted in standard class. Not permitted in first class coaches.

food_and_drink.general: Food and drink are permitted in all coaches.
food_and_drink.trolley_service: A refreshment trolley operates on selected services. Check the service schedule for availability.
food_and_drink.alcohol: Alcohol is permitted in moderation. Passengers behaving disruptively due to alcohol may be asked to leave the train.
food_and_drink.first_class: Complimentary beverages are not provided. Passengers may bring their own food and drink.

quiet_zones.description: Selected standard class carriages are designated quiet zones. Phone calls, loud music, and disruptive noise are not permitted in quiet zones.
quiet_zones.identification: Quiet zone carriages are marked with a quiet zone symbol on the door and inside the carriage.
quiet_zones.rule: Passengers should check carriage markings before boarding if a quiet journey is preferred.

priority_seating.description: Dedicated priority seats are available in all coaches, marked with a blue priority sign.
priority_seating.rule: Priority seats are reserved for elderly passengers (65+), pregnant women, passengers with disabilities, and passengers travelling with young children. Other passengers must vacate on request.

accessibility.wheelchair_access: All national rail stations are wheelchair accessible. Ramps are available on request from platform staff.
accessibility.assisted_boarding: Passengers requiring assistance should contact customer service at least 24 hours before travel.
accessibility.priority_boarding: Passengers with mobility difficulties are welcome to board first. Please inform platform staff.

prohibited_items: Items emitting strong odours that may cause discomfort to other passengers
national_rail.smoking_and_vaping: Strictly prohibited on all train carriages and at all station platforms.
national_rail.conduct: Passengers behaving in a manner that causes distress or danger to other passengers or staff may be removed from the train. In serious cases, the operator reserves the right to involve law enforcement.
